code: # New file: R/counterfactual_spec.R  #' Create counterfactual specification #' @param comparison_group Function or string defining comparison units #' @param time_periods Function or string defining time periods to use #' @param method Name of counterfactual generator create_cf_spec <- function(comparison_group, time_periods, method) {   structure(     list(       comparison_group = comparison_group,       time_periods = time_periods,       method = method     ),     class = "cf_spec"   ) }  # Convenience constructors cf_spec_cs <- function() {   create_cf_spec(     comparison_group = "not_yet_treated",  # g > t or is.na(g)     time_periods = "last_pre_period",      # t = g - 1     method = "CS"   ) }  cf_spec_imputation <- function() {   create_cf_spec(     comparison_group = "never_and_not_yet_treated",  # treated == FALSE     time_periods = "all_pre_periods",                # t < g     method = "imputation"   ) }  # Custom spec example cf_spec_custom <- function(comp_fn, time_fn, method_name) {   create_cf_spec(     comparison_group = comp_fn,  # User-supplied function     time_periods = time_fn,       # User-supplied function     method = method_name   ) }

code: # New file: R/estimator_adapters.R  #' Base class for estimator adapters #' Each adapter translates between common format and package-specific format estimator_adapter <- function(name, fit_fn, extract_fn) {   structure(     list(       name = name,       fit = fit_fn,      # Translates common args → package call       extract = extract_fn  # Translates package result → common format     ),     class = "estimator_adapter"   ) }  # Common output format standard_estimate <- function(att, se, model_name, additional_info = list()) {   list(     att = att,     se = se,     model = model_name,     info = additional_info   ) }

title: Refactoring Analysis: Modularization & Translation Layer Design
code: # R/adapters/adapter_cs.R  adapter_cs <- function() {    fit_fn <- function(data, outcome_var, time_var, id_var, group_var,                      controls, cluster_var, n_cores, ...) {     # Translate common args to CS-specific format     if(length(controls) > 0) {       control_formula <- as.formula(paste0(" ~ ", paste(controls, collapse = " + ")))     } else {       control_formula <- as.formula(" ~ 1")     }      # Call CS package     m_csa <- att_gt(       yname = outcome_var,       xformla = control_formula,       tname = time_var,       idname = id_var,       gname = group_var,       data = data,       allow_unbalanced_panel = FALSE,       panel = TRUE,       est_method = 'dr',       control_group = 'notyettreated',       clustervars = cluster_var,       cores = n_cores     )      # Aggregate     agg_csa <- aggte(m_csa, type = 'simple', na.rm = TRUE)      return(list(raw = m_csa, agg = agg_csa))   }    extract_fn <- function(result) {     # Extract to common format     standard_estimate(       att = result$agg$overall.att,       se = result$agg$overall.se,       model_name = "cs",       additional_info = list(         n_groups = length(unique(result$raw$group)),         aggregation = "simple"       )     )   }    estimator_adapter(name = "cs", fit_fn = fit_fn, extract_fn = extract_fn) }

code: # R/adapters/adapter_imputation.R  adapter_imputation <- function() {    fit_fn <- function(data, outcome_var, time_var, id_var, group_var,                      controls, cluster_var, ...) {     # Translate common args to imputation-specific format     if (!is.null(controls) && length(controls) > 0) {       imp_formula <- as.formula(paste0('~ 0 + ', paste(controls, collapse = " + ")))     } else {       imp_formula <- NULL     }      # Call didimputation package     m_imp <- did_imputation(       data = data[!is.na(get(outcome_var))],       yname = outcome_var,       gname = group_var,       tname = time_var,       idname = id_var,       first_stage = imp_formula,       cluster_var = cluster_var     )      return(list(agg = m_imp))   }    extract_fn <- function(result) {     standard_estimate(       att = result$agg$estimate,       se = result$agg$std.error,       model_name = "imputation",       additional_info = list()     )   }    estimator_adapter(name = "imputation", fit_fn = fit_fn, extract_fn = extract_fn) }

code: # R/adapters/adapter_sunab.R  adapter_sunab <- function() {    fit_fn <- function(data, outcome_var, time_var, id_var, group_var,                      controls, cluster_var, treat_ind_var, ...) {     # Translate to sunab format     if(length(controls) > 0) {       control_terms <- paste(controls, collapse = " + ")       sunab_formula <- as.formula(         paste0(outcome_var, " ~ sunab(", group_var, ", ", time_var, ") + ",                control_terms, " | ", id_var, " + ", time_var)       )     } else {       sunab_formula <- as.formula(         paste0(outcome_var, " ~ sunab(", group_var, ", ", time_var, ") | ",                id_var, " + ", time_var)       )     }      # Call fixest::sunab via feols     m_sunab <- feols(sunab_formula, data = data, cluster = cluster_var)      # Aggregate coefficients     agg_sunab <- aggregate(m_sunab, agg = "att")      return(list(raw = m_sunab, agg = agg_sunab))   }    extract_fn <- function(result) {     # Extract ATT estimate     coef_table <- summary(result$agg)$coefficients      standard_estimate(       att = coef_table[1, 1],       se = coef_table[1, 2],       model_name = "sunab",       additional_info = list()     )   }    estimator_adapter(name = "sunab", fit_fn = fit_fn, extract_fn = extract_fn) }

Available: %s",                  name, paste(names(.estimator_registry), collapse = ", ")))   }   .estimator_registry[[name]] }  #' List available estimators list_estimators <- function() {   names(.estimator_registry) }  # Register built-in adapters on package load .onLoad <- function(libname, pkgname) {   register_estimator(adapter_cs())   register_estimator(adapter_imputation())   register_estimator(adapter_sunab())  # If available }

code: # R/enforce_pta.R (refactored)  enforce_PTA <- function(df, unit, group, time, outcome, controls = NULL,                         cf_spec = NULL, seed = NULL) {    # Backward compatibility: if cf_spec is NULL, use old 'method' parameter   if (is.null(cf_spec)) {     warning("Using deprecated 'method' parameter. Please use cf_spec instead.")     method <- match.arg(method, c("imputation", "CS"))     if (method == "imputation") {       cf_spec <- cf_spec_imputation()     } else {       cf_spec <- cf_spec_cs()     }   }    # Dispatch based on cf_spec$method   if (cf_spec$method == "CS") {     enforce_PTA_CS(df, unit, group, time, outcome, controls, seed, cf_spec)   } else if (cf_spec$method == "imputation") {     enforce_PTA_imputation(df, unit, group, time, outcome, controls, seed, cf_spec)   } else {     # Custom counterfactual generator     enforce_PTA_custom(df, unit, group, time, outcome, controls, seed, cf_spec)   } }  # Updated internal functions now accept cf_spec and use it to determine # comparison groups and time periods

code: # R/estimate_models.R (refactored)  estimate_models <- function(data, id_var, outcome_var, time_var, group_var,                             controls, models_to_run, cluster_var = NULL,                             treat_ind_var = 'law_pass', n_cores = NULL, ...) {    # Defaults   if (is.null(cluster_var)) cluster_var <- id_var   if (is.null(n_cores)) n_cores <- max(1, parallel::detectCores() - 1)    all_results <- list()    # Loop over requested models   for (model_name in models_to_run) {     # Get adapter from registry     adapter <- get_estimator(model_name)      # Call adapter's fit function with common arguments     result <- adapter$fit(       data = data,       outcome_var = outcome_var,       time_var = time_var,       id_var = id_var,       group_var = group_var,       controls = controls,       cluster_var = cluster_var,       treat_ind_var = treat_ind_var,       n_cores = n_cores,       ...  # Pass through any additional args     )      all_results[[model_name]] <- result   }    return(all_results) }

code: # R/adapters/adapter_cs.R  #' @title Callaway-Sant'Anna Adapter #' @description Adapter for did::att_gt estimator adapter_cs <- function() {    fit_fn <- function(data, outcome_var, time_var, id_var, group_var,                      controls = NULL, cluster_var = NULL, n_cores = NULL,                      event_study = FALSE, ...) {      # Package check     if (!requireNamespace("did", quietly = TRUE)) {       stop("Package 'did' required for CS estimator. Install with: install.packages('did')")     }      # Defaults     if (is.null(cluster_var)) cluster_var <- id_var     if (is.null(n_cores)) n_cores <- max(1, parallel::detectCores() - 1)      # Translate controls to xformla     if (!is.null(controls) && length(controls) > 0) {       control_formula <- as.formula(paste0(" ~ ", paste(controls, collapse = " + ")))     } else {       control_formula <- as.formula(" ~ 1")     }      # Call did package     m_csa <- did::att_gt(       yname = outcome_var,       xformla = control_formula,       tname = time_var,       idname = id_var,       gname = group_var,       data = data,       allow_unbalanced_panel = FALSE,       panel = TRUE,       est_method = 'dr',       control_group = 'notyettreated',       clustervars = cluster_var,       cores = n_cores     )      # Aggregate     agg_csa <- did::aggte(m_csa, type = 'simple', na.rm = TRUE)      result <- list(       raw = m_csa,       agg = agg_csa     )      # Event study if requested     if (event_study) {       ev_csa <- did::aggte(m_csa, type = 'dynamic', na.rm = TRUE,                           min_e = -6, max_e = 10)       result$ev <- ev_csa     }      return(result)   }    extract_fn <- function(result) {     # Extract aggregated results     att <- result$agg$overall.att     se <- result$agg$overall.se      # Get diagnostics     n_groups <- length(unique(result$raw$group))     n_units <- length(unique(result$raw$id))      standard_estimate(       att = att,       se = se,       model_name = "cs",       n_units = n_units,       n_groups = n_groups,       additional_info = list(         method = "doubly-robust",         control_group = "not-yet-treated",         aggregation = "simple"       )     )   }    estimator_adapter(     name = "cs",     fit_fn = fit_fn,     extract_fn = extract_fn,     requires = "did"   ) }

code: # R/adapters/adapter_imputation.R  #' @title DID Imputation Adapter #' @description Adapter for didimputation::did_imputation estimator adapter_imputation <- function() {    fit_fn <- function(data, outcome_var, time_var, id_var, group_var,                      controls = NULL, cluster_var = NULL,                      event_study = FALSE, ...) {      # Package check     if (!requireNamespace("didimputation", quietly = TRUE)) {       stop("Package 'didimputation' required. Install with: install.packages('didimputation')")     }      # Defaults     if (is.null(cluster_var)) cluster_var <- id_var      # Translate controls to first_stage formula     if (!is.null(controls) && length(controls) > 0) {       imp_formula <- as.formula(paste0('~ 0 + ', paste(controls, collapse = " + ")))     } else {       imp_formula <- NULL     }      # Call didimputation package     m_imp <- didimputation::did_imputation(       data = data[!is.na(get(outcome_var))],       yname = outcome_var,       gname = group_var,       tname = time_var,       idname = id_var,       first_stage = imp_formula,       cluster_var = cluster_var     )      result <- list(agg = m_imp)      # Event study if requested     if (event_study) {       ev_imp <- didimputation::did_imputation(         data = data[!is.na(get(outcome_var))],         yname = outcome_var,         gname = group_var,         tname = time_var,         idname = id_var,         first_stage = imp_formula,         cluster_var = cluster_var,         horizon = TRUE,         pretrends = -6:0       )       result$ev <- ev_imp     }      return(result)   }    extract_fn <- function(result) {     # Extract aggregated results     att <- result$agg$estimate     se <- result$agg$std.error      # Get diagnostics from result     n_obs <- nrow(result$agg)      standard_estimate(       att = att,       se = se,       model_name = "imputation",       additional_info = list(         method = "imputation",         n_estimates = n_obs       )     )   }    estimator_adapter(     name = "imputation",     fit_fn = fit_fn,     extract_fn = extract_fn,     requires = "didimputation"   ) }

code: # R/estimate_models_v2.R  #' @title Estimate Models (New Adapter-Based Version) #' @description Uses adapter registry instead of hardcoded if-blocks estimate_models_v2 <- function(data, id_var, outcome_var, time_var, group_var,                                controls = NULL, models_to_run = c("cs", "imputation"),                                cluster_var = NULL, n_cores = NULL,                                event_study = FALSE, ...) {    all_results <- list()    for (model_name in models_to_run) {     # Get adapter from registry     adapter <- get_estimator(model_name)      # Check package requirements     if (!is.null(adapter$requires)) {       if (!requireNamespace(adapter$requires, quietly = TRUE)) {         warning(sprintf("Skipping %s: package '%s' not installed",                        model_name, adapter$requires))         next       }     }      # Call adapter     result <- adapter$fit(       data = data,       outcome_var = outcome_var,       time_var = time_var,       id_var = id_var,       group_var = group_var,       controls = controls,       cluster_var = cluster_var,       n_cores = n_cores,       event_study = event_study,       ...     )      all_results[[model_name]] <- result   }    return(all_results) }

what: Why power analysis for staggered DiD?
title: Getting Started with staggeredpower
text: Staggered difference--differences () designs common policy evaluation, different units adopt treatment different times. Modern heterogeneity-robust estimators (Callaway & Sant’Anna 2021, Borusyak et al. 2024, Gardner 2022) address bias treatment effect heterogeneity, statistical power properties less well understood. staggeredpower fills gap simulating data-generating process controlled conditions: enforces parallel trends actual data, injects treatment effects specified size, estimates models compute rejection rates. lets answer: “Given panel structure, large must effect estimator detect ?”

title: Power Analysis for Staggered Difference-in-Differences Designs
text: Use run_power_analysis() estimate power one set parameters. : 1. Generates counterfactual outcomes parallel trends assumption 2. Simulates treatment multipliers specified magnitude 3. Estimates models calculates statistical significance rates Data requirements: data panel dataset : - Unit identifier (e.g., state_fips, county_name) - Time variable (e.g., year) - Treatment cohort (e.g., year_passed - year unit adopted treatment; use 0 NA never-treated units) - Treatment indicator (e.g., law_pass - 0 , 1 adoption) - Outcome variable(s) - Relative time treatment (e.g., rel_pass = year - year_passed) Balanced panels required. default, package follows underlying estimator defaults assumes balanced panel Callaway-Sant’Anna estimation. panel unbalanced, pass allow_unbalanced_panel = TRUE; forwarded ::att_gt() CS specifications. estimators may support requirements, check estimator-specific warnings missing-result rows. Example: Key parameters: "cs": Callaway-Sant’Anna approach (recommended) "imputation": Two-way fixed effects imputation percent_effect = 1.00 means effect percent_effect = 0.90 means 10% decline percent_effect = 1.10 means 10% increase 100 quick estimates, 500+ publication results noise_spec = list(engine = "none"), simulations deterministic package sets n_sims = 1 Used PTA enforcement estimation Default FALSE, matching ::att_gt() Set TRUE unbalanced state-year agency-year panels Default list(engine = "none"), deterministic benchmark Use list(engine = "iid"), list(engine = "ar1"), observation models obs_model = "poisson" / "binomial" stochastic power calculations Interpreting results: Power 0.82 means 82% chance detecting effect 5% significance level.
code: library(data.table) library(staggeredpower)  # Load your data # data <- fread("your_data.csv")  results <- run_power_analysis(   data_clean = data,    # Panel structure   unit_var = "state_fips",        # Unit ID column name   group_var = "year_passed",      # Treatment cohort column name   time_var = "year",              # Time column name   rel_pass_var = "rel_pass",      # Relative time column name   treat_ind_var = "law_pass",     # Treatment indicator column name    # Outcome   outcome = "dv_rate",            # Outcome column name    # PTA enforcement method   pta_type = "cs",                # "cs" or "imputation"    # Simulated treatment multiplier   percent_effect = 0.90,          # Simulate a 10% decline (post-treatment outcomes are 90% of counterfactual)    # Number of simulations   n_sims = 100,                   # Number of Monte Carlo draws    # Optional: controls and estimators   controls = c("unemp_rate"),   models_to_run = c("cs", "imputation"),    # Optional: unbalanced panels and stochastic outcome noise   allow_unbalanced_panel = TRUE,   noise_spec = list(engine = "iid") ) # Results structure names(results) # [1] "final_power"  "final_vio"  # Main results: one row per simulation head(results$final_power)  # Calculate power by estimator power_by_model <- results$final_power[, .(   power = mean(abs(att/se) > 1.96),     # Share rejecting null at 5%   mean_att = mean(att),                  # Average estimated effect   mean_se = mean(se),                    # Average standard error   mean_bound_errors = mean(n_bound_errors),   mean_violating_units = mean(n_violating_units) ), by = model]  print(power_by_model) #    model power mean_att mean_se mean_bound_errors mean_violating_units # 1:    cs  0.82    -0.51    0.24              0.0                  0.0 # 2: imputation 0.76    -0.48    0.26              0.0                  0.0

title: Power Analysis for Staggered Difference-in-Differences Designs
text: Use run_power_grid() test power across many parameter combinations. use: want explore power changes across different treatment multipliers, PTA methods, control specifications. recommend approach Example - Testing multiple treatment multipliers: Example - Testing multiple control sets: Grid search output: Visualizing power curves:
code: grid_results <- run_power_grid(   data_clean = data,   unit_var = "state_fips",   group_var = "year_passed",   time_var = "year",   rel_pass_var = "rel_pass",   treat_ind_var = "law_pass",   outcome = "dv_rate",    # Vectors of parameters to test   pta_type = c("cs", "imputation"),   percent_effect = seq(0.80, 1.00, 0.05),  # Test 20%, 15%, 10%, 5%, 0% declines    n_sims = 100 )  # View power summary print(grid_results$power_summary) grid_results <- run_power_grid(   data_clean = data,   unit_var = "state_fips",   group_var = "year_passed",   time_var = "year",   rel_pass_var = "rel_pass",   treat_ind_var = "law_pass",   outcome = "dv_rate",    # Test different control combinations   controls = list(     NULL,                           # No controls     c("unemp_rate"),               # Unemployment only     c("unemp_rate", "crime_rate")  # Both controls   ),    percent_effect = c(0.80, 0.85, 0.90),   n_sims = 100 )  # Results contain all combinations # 3 control sets × 3 effect sizes = 9 specifications names(grid_results) # [1] "final_power"      "power_summary"    "specifications"  # final_power: Individual simulation results for all specs # power_summary: Aggregated power statistics by specification # specifications: Grid of all parameter combinations tested library(ggplot2)  ggplot(grid_results$power_summary,        aes(x = percent_effect, y = power, color = pta_type)) +   geom_line() +   geom_point() +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0.80, linetype = "dashed") +   labs(title = "Statistical Power by Effect Size",        x = "Treatment Multiplier",        y = "Power",        color = "PTA Method") +   theme_minimal()

title: Power Analysis for Staggered Difference-in-Differences Designs
text: final_power table: Contains one row per simulation columns: - att: Estimated treatment effect - se: Standard error - model: estimator (“cs”, “imputation”, “twfe”) - percent_effect: Simulated treatment multiplier - n_bound_errors: Number counterfactual draws feasible lower bound clipping - n_violating_units: Number units diagnostic bound missing-counterfactual issues - noise_engine: Noise engine used simulation - obs_model: Observation model used stochastic draws final_vio table: Legacy diagnostics parallel-trends/bound violations. current versions, bound violations tracked diagnostically units dropped automatically power simulations. PTA bound diagnostics: CS imputation PTA generators can produce infeasible missing counterfactuals sparse panels noisy outcomes. Current versions treat diagnostics rather automatically dropping units: - n_bound_errors: number counterfactual values zero clipping - n_violating_units: number units bound missing-counterfactual diagnostics Many diagnostics may indicate fragile specification, even estimator still returns results.
code: # Summarize frequent violators violation_summary <- results$final_vio[, .N, by = .(unit, iteration)][, .(   times_violated = .N ), by = unit][order(-times_violated)]  print(violation_summary)

title: Enforce Parallel Trends Assumption — enforce_PTA
text: Three methods available: imputation Uses two-way fixed effects untreated observations impute counterfactuals. Assumes additive parallel trends level scale. CS Uses Callaway-Sant'Anna style approach -yet-treated controls. Assumes additive parallel trends level scale. poisson Uses Poisson regression unit time fixed effects untreated observations. Assumes multiplicative parallel trends log-rate scale. appropriate rare count outcomes (Wooldridge 2023).

title: staggeredpower 0.1.0
text: Initial CRAN release. Core functions: run_power_analysis(), run_power_grid(), estimate_models(). Pluggable estimator adapters: Callaway-Sant’Anna (adapter_cs), Gardner 2SDID (adapter_did2s), Borusyak-Jaravel-Spiess imputation (adapter_imputation), ETWFE Poisson (adapter_etwfe_poisson_glm). Parallel trends enforcement via enforce_PTA() imputation, CS, Poisson methods. Configurable noise engines: none (deterministic), iid, ar1, ar1_common, ar1_anchored. Pre-trend diagnostic testing compute_pretrend_wald_test(). Custom adapter registration via register_adapter(). YAML-based configuration load_config(). Optional parallel execution via foreach/doParallel automatic lapply fallback.
